title: Allow Viewers to comment
excerpt: The idea of Viewers is great. I never want a viewer to be able to update anything. But if they have a question, or an updated piece of information, rather than forcing them to message me over teams or email, just allow t…

posts.author: mmecham (Mary Mecham)
posts.cookedHtml: The idea of Viewers is great. I never want a viewer to be able to update anything. But if they have a question, or an updated piece of information, rather than forcing them to message me over teams or email, just allow them to “Add to the Conversation”. Don’t block that feature. We should be able to have a dialogue with Viewers in the platform when they have questions or needs.

posts.author: Dolph (Dolph Himmelbauer)
posts.cookedHtml: The ability for “Viewers” to add to comments would be immeasurable to our New Product Development space. We have hundreds of sales and service people that I would like to have the ability to interact with in this space, but don’t want them to have the ability to change structure or add/delete items, nor would it be affordable or useful to make them “Members.” So, this is a big gap in the effectiveness of the system. I need to find a workaround that doesn’t force us back into emails or suggest to so many that Monday.com is not the right choice of tools…

posts.author: Dolph (Dolph Himmelbauer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Use Case: How we use Monday.com now
• We’ve recently created a New Product Development (NPD) board for our company in Monday.com. Previously, this, along with our QCR/ECR systems, resided in the company’s internal network site. One of the limitations to this was the difficulty integrating this with our project management tools, as well as the limitations on collaborating with the requestors to truly understand the need for such projects/developments. Extensive use of emails makes this process more difficult, since not all email chains get routed to the right sources at the right times. Most of the NPD’s that come in originate from our sales and service folks around the globe, upwards of 150 people and consistently growing. We’ve made the entry of requests very easy by adding the form to our network site, however when we need to get more info from them we have to resort back to emails… Due to the way we’ve set up the Form, the need to get more information for an NPD Request doesn’t happen every day or with every request. So, buying additional licenses for this would not be cost justifiable.
Feature Request in the context of our pain
• The feature we would like to request is a method whereby a “Viewer” can simply add comments to the Updates section of an item. We don’t want them to have the ability to delete anything or add to any item content…, just to be able to answer a question posed to them for clarification… Additionally, if they can attach a file, that would be helpful as a picture can speak volumes.
Impact on our team and adoption of Monday
• We recently just increased our licenses in the Enterprise package from 50 to 65 Members. We also just recently held our Global Kickoff for 2023, where Monday.com received considerable attention. So, while there has been significant focus placed on the importance of Monday.com to our project management and new product development processes, our budgets are locked into a number of these and will not likely include expansion of our licenses for Monday again soon. The issue is, at this early adoption phase of Monday.com, if our sales service team can’t at least comment / answer questions inside our source of truth (Monday.com), it will quickly lose its luster with that group and we’ll be forced back into the email vortex.
Workarounds and their impacts
• As mentioned above, the only work around I’ve found so far is going back to emails. This slows our process, de-emphasizes the value of Monday.com and risks the propagation of using Monday.com for more applications within our business.
• A second potential work around that I’m testing now is to create an Online Doc, share access in Google Drive and see if Viewers can add anything. If this works, one additional issue this will create is the need for more training and encouragement for these non-Monday users to use Monday. The more difficult we make it and the more steps we add, the less they will use it, especially since their primary objective is to SELL…

posts.author: Borderc21 (Chet Borden)
posts.cookedHtml: A very similar topic here: Require another user type between 'Member' and 'Viewer'. Go vote!

posts.author: susibarreto (Susi)
posts.cookedHtml: This is something we are also facing. There has to be a way around this. has anyone find some solutions?

posts.author: bob1 (bob)
posts.cookedHtml: I second this request. We have many “viewer” execs who would sometimes like to add comments on certain tasks. However, we’re on an enterprise plan so purchasing additional full price licences just to let them comment on a few tasks is simply not worth it.

posts.author: Universe (Universe)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update from Monday if this is in the pipeline of features or something can be done on modifying a subscription option for “Viewer but can comment”? Really need this feature!

posts.author: cramos (Christian Ramos)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this? I spoke to a monday.com rep and they said they were going to add a vote for every member on our account. Not seeing that reflected here. We would find this incredibly useful at our company.

posts.author: mierendorf (Michael Mierendorf)
posts.cookedHtml: Adding another vote and support for this. We have 60 member licenses, but share boards with more than 70 others as viewers. Sometimes all they want to do is leave a comment!

posts.author: cgodbou (Charles Godbout)
posts.cookedHtml:
Helllo

I aggreed that it would be awesome to have such possibility.
+1 vote

Thanks !!

posts.author: lawsonmy (Myisha )
posts.cookedHtml: This feature is needed for executives and managers. They will not use nor need to use features, however, they view reports and need to be able to comment. Thanks.

posts.author: jamiejo (Jamie Thomson)
posts.cookedHtml:
I want to express how important it is for viewers to be able to comment on items. While I understand the need to limit editing access, commenting is a critical part of collaboration—especially in large organizations where many team members are stakeholders, but not direct board editors.

Allowing viewers to leave comments:

  • Keeps communication centralized and visible in context
  • Reduces the need for email or Slack back-and-forth
  • Encourages engagement from all collaborators without compromising board structure

This would be a huge win for transparency and communication. Please consider prioritizing this feature—it would make a meaningful difference to how we use Monday.com across departments.

posts.author: chrisgeorgiou46 (Chris Georgiou)
posts.cookedHtml: I am going to second what everyone else is saying. We have a high number of people that are currently viewers, wanting to leave the occasional comment. We will never get signoff to add these people as paid members because again they will only occasional comment. It severely limits our ability to collaborate with the wider company. Would appreciate an update that this is being looked into.

posts.author: cramos (Christian Ramos)
posts.cookedHtml: @barry Updated here? I’ve seen features created based on these forums with votes. Clearly people want this. There has to be a workaround for this. All my viewers do not need full licenses when the only feature they will use is a comment here and there

posts.author: kmachin (Kelly Machin)
posts.cookedHtml: Please add this feature. It so incredibly frustrating for us to navigate the “send update via email, as the only way to communicate with Viewer level users.

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @mmecham ,

We’ve seen this need come up often when aligning project timelines with clients on a Gantt. It often turns into an endless feedback loop and version chaos:

export → send PDF → wait → clarify in email → update → resend → repeat.

With Interactive Gantt Timeline yoг can share Gantt with clients and collaborate directly on it:

  • Clients can leave comments and suggest timeline changes right on Gantt

    leave suggestions2045×1045 312 KB

  • PMs get notification about comments and suggestions, and can test different scenarios, and then approve the appropriate timeline.

    test versions gantt1581×993 212 KB

After approving, new timeline automatically updates the board.

Interactive Gantt Timeline doesn’t replace monday.com’s native Gantt — it enhances it.
Keep using monday.com’s Gantt view internally, and share Interactive Gantt Timeline with clients outside your workspace.

You can install and test the app on your account.
